intro: Maxillaria minus, also known as 'Hooded Max', is a species of orchid native to the tropical rainforests of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Maxillaria minus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
